entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company, positioned as a different data-collection layer rather than an 'AI ATS'

entities.name: Operator to fund manager narrative
entities.context: Career arc pattern: operator success → SPV wins → fund raise based on track record

entities.name: SPVs
entities.context: Special Purpose Vehicles used for individual deals before raising a formal fund

entities.name: Fund math
entities.context: How large funds calculate required returns - deals must have potential for $500M+ returns

entities.name: Preference stack
entities.context: Investor preferences that can leave founders with zero in many exit scenarios despite years of work

entities.name: Mineral extraction metaphor
entities.context: V's framing that 99% of résumé ore is worthless but must still be processed - AI on résumés alone cannot be accurate and economically rational

entities.name: LinkedIn
entities.context: Source of inbound applications; Jonathan's team came from LinkedIn after ~1,500 applications per role

relationships.context: Spun out Zoombini as part of his operator background
Did successful SPVs before raising formal fund
Hired both current team members through LinkedIn inbound
Won't do deals unless return potential is $500M+
V's core critique of ATS/résumé-based hiring
Careerspan positioned as solution - different data-collection layer vs AI on bad data
SPV wins are stepping stone in the operator-to-fund-manager arc
